description_long: This is a tall, vertically-oriented storage tank with a cylindrical body and a rounded, domed top. The tank features a prominent pipe or vent extending from the top, likely for pressure release or ventilation. The lower section includes a complex assembly of valves and pipework, suggesting it is designed for controlled dispensing or filling of liquids or gases. The body of the tank is mostly smooth, with some vent-like cutouts near the upper section, possibly for airflow or inspection. The rear side appears to have a large, flat access panel or maintenance hatch. The overall construction is robust, indicating it is built to withstand high internal pressures and outdoor urban conditions.
description: A tall, cylindrical storage tank with a domed top, pipe attachments, and a valve assembly at the base. It features a smooth body with vent cutouts and a rear access panel.
description_view_0: Front view shows the domed top, central pipe, and the main valve assembly at the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the cylindrical profile and the protruding pipe at the top.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals a large, flat access panel and the continuation of the top pipe.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays the smooth cylindrical body and the upper vent cutouts.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
